essential_question: Plans on paper, proof in your hands — can you make all three pieces real?
objective: Carry out the three-part capstone build: take the trig measurement, build/print the solid, and perform the coordinate-plane transformation — recording every measurement as you go.

content.heading: From plan to proof
bullets: Yesterday you planned all three parts of your capstone.
Today you make each one real and record the numbers as you go — nothing counts until it's measured.

content.heading: Height or distance, by angle
bullets: Measure the angle of elevation (or depression) and the baseline distance.
Compute the height or distance using the correct trig ratio.
Record your work — the angle, the baseline, the equation, the answer.

content.heading: Build it, then measure it
bullets: Build or print the solid from your plan.
Measure its real dimensions with a ruler.
Compute volume and surface area from those measured dimensions.

agenda.title: Build — Part 3: Transform on the Coordinate Plane

content.heading: Move it, then prove it moved correctly
bullets: Plot your original figure and label every vertex's coordinates.
Plot and label the image's coordinates.

content.preview: Session 3: write it all up for the Portfolio, with a short proof.

success_criteria.text: I can measure an angle and baseline distance and compute a real height or distance using trig
I can build/print a solid, measure its dimensions, and compute its volume and surface area
I can perform a transformation on the coordinate plane and record the original and image coordinates

questions.prompt: Record your Part 1 numbers: angle of elevation, baseline distance, and the computed height/distance (show the trig equation you used).
questions.answer: Any consistent measured angle + baseline distance + computed result, with the trig equation shown.

questions.prompt: Record your Part 2 numbers: the solid's measured dimensions and your computed volume and surface area.
questions.answer: Any consistent measured dimensions plus computed volume/surface area, with formulas shown.

questions.prompt: Record your Part 3 numbers: the original figure's vertices and the image's vertices after your transformation.
questions.answer: Any consistent set of before/after coordinate pairs matching the transformation performed.

questions.prompt: Reflect: which of the three parts took the most rework today, and what would you do differently if you built it again?
